61.reason: [Error: This increments i before null termination, causing the null terminator to overwrite the last character. Should be c[i+1]='\0' or c[i]='\0' after the loop.]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: This increments i before null termination, causing the null terminator to overwrite the last character. Should be c[i+1]='\0' or c[i]='\0' after the loop.]
reasons.buggy-deepseek-chat-2: 1.0 , reason:  [Error: This increments i before null-terminating, causing the null terminator to overwrite the last character. Should be c[i+1]='\0' or c[i]='\0' after the loop without incrementing i again.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  Potential buffer overflow if the word length reaches maxword. Also, modifying i here affects the subsequent buffer clearing loop logic.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 1.0 , reason:  [Buffer overflow: 'i' may reach 'maxword-1', then 'c[++i]' writes beyond array bounds. Also, logic error: should be 'c[i+1]='\0'' after loop without incrementing i again.]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [off-by-one error: after loop, i points to last valid char; incrementing before null-terminator writes beyond intended position]

50.reason: Declaration after statement; not allowed in C89 (gcc default may be strict)

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 1.0 , reason:  Declaration after statement; not allowed in C89 (gcc default may be strict)

19.reason: r may be uninitialized if list not empty but p becomes NULL before loop executes? Actually r is only set if loop executes at least once; if list has one node and it's less than q, loop runs once, r gets p (which is list), then p becomes NULL. That's fine. However, later uses of r assume it was initialized.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  r may be uninitialized if list not empty but p becomes NULL before loop executes? Actually r is only set if loop executes at least once; if list has one node and it's less than q, loop runs once, r gets p (which is list), then p becomes NULL. That's fine. However, later uses of r assume it was initialized.

32.reason: p could be NULL here when the new word is lexicographically larger than all existing words, causing a segmentation fault. Need to check p != NULL before calling strcmp.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  p can be NULL (if all words less than q), leading to null pointer dereference.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  p may be NULL after the loop, causing a segmentation fault when passed to strcmp. Should check p != NULL first.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 1.0 , reason:  p could be NULL here when the new word is lexicographically larger than all existing words, causing a segmentation fault. Need to check p != NULL before calling strcmp.

45.reason: ch should be declared as int to correctly handle EOF returned by fgetc, otherwise it may cause an infinite loop or incorrect termination on platforms where char is unsigned.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  'ch' should be declared as 'int' to properly detect EOF from fgetc().
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  ch should be declared as int to correctly handle EOF returned by fgetc, otherwise it may cause an infinite loop or incorrect termination on platforms where char is unsigned.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [char ch should be int to properly handle EOF, as fgetc returns int]

47.reason: [fgetc returns int; storing in char may cause EOF comparison failure on systems where char is unsigned]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  'ch' is char; fgetc returns int. If char is signed, EOF (-1) may be misinterpreted.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.25 , reason:  [fgetc returns int; storing in char may cause EOF comparison failure on systems where char is unsigned]
